one_line_takeaway: A well-organized survey plus a single-harness (TruthTorchLM) benchmark of 16 UQ methods for LLM hallucination detection, showing trained methods lead on short-form QA but no method generalizes across tasks, models, and access levels, with all methods degrading on long-form generation.
key_finding: Supervised/trained methods (LARS, SAPLMA) top short-form QA when labeled data is available: on LLaMA-3-8B TriviaQA, LARS reaches AUROC 0.861 / PRR 0.783 and SAPLMA 0.850 / 0.726. Among self-supervised methods, SAR is best on LLaMA-3-8B short-form (TriviaQA 0.804/0.679, GSM8K 0.768/0.590). Rankings shift by model: for GPT-4o-mini, Verbalized Confidence wins TriviaQA (0.836/0.740) and Multi-LLM Collab wins GSM8K (0.933/0.879). Long-form (FactScore-Bio) is harder and scores drop across the board; Verbalized Confidence is best on both models there (LLaMA-3-8B 0.698/0.460, GPT-4o-mini 0.717/0.514). Takeaway: no single UQ method dominates across tasks, models, and access levels, and performance degrades markedly from short- to long-form QA.
core_contribution: A survey (with an accompanying original empirical study) of uncertainty quantification (UQ) as a tool for hallucination detection in LLMs on open-ended QA. The authors' OWN contributions are: (1) a consolidation of UQ foundations, connecting the classical aleatoric/epistemic decomposition (via entropy of the posterior predictive distribution and pointwise Bayes risk) to the auto-regressive, open-ended LLM setting, and arguing that correctness-based evaluation of UQ effectively measures epistemic uncertainty; (2) a four-axis taxonomy that positions 30+ existing UQ methods along conceptual approach (token-probability / output-consistency / internal-state-examination / self-checking), sampling requirement (single vs. multiple), model accessibility (black-/gray-/white-box), and training reliance (supervised vs. unsupervised) (Table 1); (3) a unified empirical re-evaluation of 16 representative methods under one harness (TruthTorchLM); and (4) a limitations/future-directions discussion. Note: the individual methods it catalogs (LARS, SAR, MARS, Semantic Entropy, SAPLMA, HaloScope, P(True), Verbalized Confidence, etc.) are CITED prior work, not contributions of this paper; the paper's value-add is the organizing framework plus the apples-to-apples comparison.
threat_model: None. This is a reliability/trustworthiness survey, not an adversarial-security paper. Hallucination here means the model producing plausible-but-factually-incorrect or unfaithful outputs due to knowledge gaps or ambiguity, not an attacker-induced failure. There is no adversary, attack surface, or defense in the paper's framing, so no threat model is imposed.
limitations: Stated by the authors: (1) LLM knowledge boundaries and temporal facts mean static UQ scores cannot flag outputs that become outdated; (2) most UQ scores are non-interpretable, lying on arbitrary scales rather than a [0,1] correctness probability, and calibration needs reference data; (3) principled ensemble-based epistemic UQ is infeasible because training multiple LLMs is prohibitively expensive; (4) methods are largely heuristic without clear ties to UQ theory or explicit aleatoric/epistemic separation; (5) long-form/claim-level UQ remains under-explored. Reviewer-noted scope limits: the empirical study covers only 2 models and 3 datasets at 1,000 samples each; it deliberately excludes interactive-agent and adaptive-mitigation settings; and single-run numbers are reported without variance/significance.

quantitative_results: LARS (supervised) best short-form: LLaMA-3-8B TriviaQA AUROC 0.861 / PRR 0.783; GSM8K 0.834 / 0.719
SAPLMA (supervised, white-box) LLaMA-3-8B TriviaQA AUROC 0.850 / PRR 0.726; GSM8K 0.815 / 0.642
SAR best self-supervised on LLaMA-3-8B: TriviaQA 0.804 / 0.679; GSM8K 0.768 / 0.590
GPT-4o-mini TriviaQA best = Verbalized Confidence AUROC 0.836 / PRR 0.740
GPT-4o-mini GSM8K best = Multi-LLM Collab AUROC 0.933 / PRR 0.879
FactScore-Bio (long-form) best = Verbalized Confidence: LLaMA-3-8B 0.698 / 0.460, GPT-4o-mini 0.717 / 0.514
Weakest method: Attention Scores near-random on LLaMA-3-8B GSM8K (AUROC 0.503 / PRR -0.024)
AUROC scale: 0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect; PRR: 0.0 = random rejection, 1.0 = oracle
